title: Social Confidence Builder Checklist
description: Track your progress through the key social anxiety recovery skills. Check off each milestone as you achieve it. This checklist helps you see how far you've come.
instructions: Work through these items at your own pace. Check off each one as you genuinely feel you've accomplished it. Revisit weekly to track your progress. Not every item needs to be checked — focus on the areas most relevant to your experience.

items.label: I can explain the difference between social anxiety, shyness, and introversion
I can describe my personal social anxiety cycle (before, during, after)
I can name my most common cognitive distortions (spotlight effect, mind-reading, etc.)
I've identified my safety behaviors and know which ones I use most
I've successfully challenged an automatic negative thought using evidence
I have balanced alternatives for my top 3 automatic negative thoughts
I've caught myself ruminating after a social event and redirected my attention
I've created a fear hierarchy with at least 10 items rated by SUDS
I've completed at least 3 mild exposure exercises (SUDS 20-40)
I've completed at least 3 moderate exposure exercises (SUDS 40-60)
I've deliberately dropped a safety behavior during a social situation
I've completed a behavioral experiment and my prediction was wrong
I've practiced external attention training in a social conversation
I've used the curiosity approach during a conversation
I've started a conversation with someone I don't know well
I've expressed my opinion in a group setting
I've been assertive about a preference or boundary

disclaimer: This checklist is for tracking your personal progress through the social anxiety course. It is not a clinical measure. If you are in crisis, contact the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline by calling or texting 988.
